children.text: Compassionate hearing care in Georgetown, KY. We are dedicated to helping you hear every meaningful moment of life.

data.hero_heading: Compassionate Hearing Care in Georgetown, KY
data.hero_subtext: Hearing care you can trust, for every stage of life.
data.about_text:
Life is full of meaningful sounds: laughter with family, conversations with friends, music you love, and the quiet moments that bring calm. When hearing or balance problems begin to interfere, it can feel isolating and overwhelming.

At Commonwealth Audiology, your hearing wellness is our top priority. Every recommendation we make is shaped by your everyday listening needs and the moments that bring meaning to your life.

Our Doctor of Audiology, Dr. Tiffany Brewer, stays up to date with the latest hearing technology so we can offer you the most effective and innovative options available.

Our care doesn't end when you choose hearing aids. We provide support each step of the way—from helping you adjust to your hearing aids, fine-tuning your device, and making sure they truly support your daily life.
data.team_text:
At Commonwealth Audiology, our focus is on restoring clarity, comfort, and confidence so you can fully re-engage with the world around you.

With expert guidance, clinical excellence, and care tailored to your listening needs, we help you hear and live better each day. Our Georgetown, KY audiologist & team takes the time to understand your goals, your listening environments, and the moments that matter most.
data.services_subtext: At Commonwealth Audiology, we provide hearing care to meet your every need. It's our commitment to make sure you can hear every moment clearly.
data.hearing_aids_subtext: We provide best-in-class hearing aid solutions with expert fittings, adjustments, repairs, and ongoing maintenance so you can hear your best every day.
data.protection_heading: Hearing Protection Solutions in Georgetown, KY
data.protection_subtext: For anyone exposed to loud noise, custom ear protection offers the safest and most comfortable way to preserve your hearing.

data.footer_tagline: Compassionate hearing care in Georgetown, KY. Helping you hear every meaningful moment of life.

data.title: Hearing Evaluations
data.description: A comprehensive hearing evaluation is the first step toward better hearing. Our Doctor of Audiology will assess your hearing health using the latest diagnostic tools and techniques.

data.title: Hearing Loss Evaluation & Treatment
data.description: We work together to understand what you are experiencing and recommend treatment options tailored to your specific type and degree of hearing loss with confidence.

data.title: Ear Wax Removal
data.description: Excess earwax can sometimes accumulate and cause hearing loss. Our safe, professional ear wax removal services restore your hearing clarity quickly and comfortably.

data.title: Tinnitus Evaluations & Treatment
data.description: Ringing, buzzing, or whooshing in the ears can be stressful. We fully evaluate your tinnitus and discuss various treatment options designed to find what works best for you.

data.title: Hearing Aid Selection
data.description: We offer a full line of hearing aid styles, including completely-in-canal and Bluetooth-compatible models, allowing you to select the style that fits your budget, lifestyle, and hearing needs.

data.title: Hearing Aid Fittings
data.description: We take great care with precise new adjustments, using real-ear measurement to ensure you can be confident and comfortable with your hearing aids in any situation.

data.title: Hearing Aid Cleaning & Repair
data.description: We provide cleaning, troubleshooting, and simple repairs so your devices work as comfortably and as well as the day you received them, getting you back to better hearing fast.

data.title: Hearing Aid Batteries & Accessories
data.description: We carry a variety of batteries, chargers, cleaning tools, and accessories to keep your hearing devices running and sounding their very best.

data.name: Dr. Tiffany Brewer, Au.D.
data.bio: Dr. Tiffany Brewer is a compassionate, knowledgeable audiologist committed to providing personalized hearing care to every patient in Georgetown, KY.

data.name: Aubrey Gingrich
data.bio: Aubrey serves as Patient Care Coordinator at Commonwealth Audiology, bringing a strong background in healthcare leadership and communication.

data.name: Magnolia
data.bio: Magnolia is a beloved member of the Commonwealth Audiology team, known for her warm and enthusiastic greetings that make every patient feel welcome.

data.question: How do I know if I need a hearing aid?
data.answer: If you frequently ask people to repeat themselves, have difficulty hearing in noisy environments, or find yourself turning up the TV volume, it may be time for a hearing evaluation. Schedule an appointment with our audiologist for a comprehensive assessment.

data.question: Are hearing aids covered by insurance?
data.answer: Coverage varies by insurance plan. Many plans offer partial coverage for hearing aids. Our team can help you understand your benefits and explore financing options to make hearing care accessible for you.

data.question: How often should I get a hearing test?
data.answer: Adults should have their hearing tested every 3-5 years, or more frequently if you notice changes. Those with existing hearing loss or high noise exposure should be tested annually.

data.question: Can I get a hearing aid without a fitting?
data.answer: A professional fitting is essential for optimal hearing aid performance. Proper fitting ensures the device is programmed correctly for your specific hearing loss, fits comfortably, and delivers the best possible sound quality.

data.question: How can hearing aids help with tinnitus?
data.answer: Many modern hearing aids include tinnitus masking features that produce soothing sounds to reduce the perception of ringing. Combined with hearing amplification, they can significantly improve quality of life for tinnitus sufferers.

data.title: Custom Hearing Protection & Ear Molds
data.description: Our custom ear solutions reduce harmful sounds while keeping important sounds audible, so you can work safely and enjoy what you love without fear of hearing damage.

data.title: Hearing Protection for Musicians
data.description: Musicians are at high risk for regular exposure to loud volumes that can harm their hearing. We provide specially designed protection that lets you enjoy music you love while preserving your hearing for years to come.

data.title: Hearing Protection for Hunters
data.description: Sudden, loud blasts from firearms can cause severe, permanent hearing damage. Our custom hunter's ear protection provides incredible ambient sound awareness while protecting your hearing from dangerous impulse noise.

data.title: About Commonwealth Audiology
data.meta_description: Commonwealth Audiology is an audiology and hearing aid clinic with an expert audiologist providing hearing tests and professional hearing services in Georgetown, KY.

children.text: Commonwealth Audiology is an audiology and hearing aid clinic with an expert audiologist providing hearing tests and professional hearing services in Georgetown, KY.

At Commonwealth Audiology, we believe better hearing brings people closer to their families, their communities, and the moments that matter most. Our practice was built on a simple promise: to deliver exceptional hearing care with the warmth, honesty, and personalized attention every patient deserves.
Here, you're never just a number or a set of test results. You're part of our patient family, and we are committed to helping you hear with clarity, comfort, and confidence every step of the way.
Personalized Care, Every Time
Knowledgeable

When you come to Commonwealth Audiology, you can feel confident you're in experienced hands. We stay current on the latest research, technology, and best practices so you receive care that is accurate, thoughtful, and tailored to you. From your first evaluation to ongoing care, every recommendation is made with your specific needs in mind.
Passionate

Helping people hear better is truly why we do what we do. There's nothing more rewarding than seeing someone reconnect with family, enjoy conversations again, or feel more like themselves. We're invested in your journey and celebrate those moments right alongside you.
Accountable

Your trust means everything to us. We take the time to recommend what genuinely fits your lifestyle and goals, not just what's available. And we're here for the long run, providing continued support and adjustments so you always feel confident in your hearing and your care.
What To Expect At Commonwealth Audiology
Your Hearing Comes First

From the moment you walk in, our focus is on you. We take the time to listen to your concerns, answer your questions, and help you truly understand your hearing—what's going on, how it's affecting your daily life, and what options will work best for you. We want you to feel comfortable, informed, and confident every step of the way.
Comprehensive Diagnostic Testing

Your evaluation is thorough and tailored, not rushed. We use precise, evidence-based testing to understand not only how your ears hear, but how your brain processes sound. This allows us to create recommendations that are thoughtfully matched to your lifestyle, communication needs, and goals.
State-of-the-Art Technology & Custom Programming

Choosing hearing aids is only part of the process—how they're programmed makes all the difference. We work with the latest technology and take the time to fine-tune your devices so they sound natural, feel comfortable, and perform well in your everyday environments. Our goal is to eliminate the frustration that can come with poorly programmed hearing aids and replace it with clarity and confidence.
Ongoing Support for Long-Term Success

Our relationship doesn't end after your fitting. We're here for you with ongoing care, including follow-up visits, adjustments, cleanings, and guidance whenever you need it. As your hearing or needs change, we'll be right there to help you adapt and continue hearing your best.
Even if you didn't purchase your hearing aids from us, you're always welcome here. We're happy to evaluate, service, and support your devices because everyone deserves thoughtful, professional care.
We Love What We Do

There's nothing more meaningful to us than seeing our patients reconnect—with conversations, with loved ones, and with the sounds that make life full. Being part of that journey is why we do what we do every day.
Experience the Difference at Commonwealth Audiology
Expert care. A family-like atmosphere. Unmatched dedication to your well-being.
Whether you're seeking a hearing evaluation, exploring hearing aid options, or looking for ongoing support, we're here to guide you with compassion, clarity, and trusted expertise.
Schedule an appointment
at Commonwealth Audiology, where your hearing health is our highest priority!

children.text: At Commonwealth Audiology, your hearing health is our priority. We're here to guide you with compassionate care, personalized support, and trusted expertise, so you can stay connected to the people and moments that matter most.

data.title: 5 Signs You May Need a Hearing Test
data.excerpt: Hearing loss often develops gradually. Here are the key signs to watch for and when to schedule your evaluation.

children.text: Hearing loss often develops so gradually that many people don't notice it until it begins significantly affecting their daily life. Knowing the warning signs can help you seek care before the problem worsens.
1. Asking people to repeat themselves
If you frequently ask friends, family, or coworkers to repeat what they've said, this is one of the most common early signs of hearing loss. It may be happening more than you realize.
2. Difficulty hearing in noisy environments

Struggling to follow conversations in restaurants, social gatherings, or other noisy places is a hallmark sign of hearing difficulty. Background noise can be especially challenging for people with hearing loss.

data.title: How Modern Hearing Aid Technology Has Changed Lives
data.excerpt: Today's hearing aids are more advanced than ever — smaller, smarter, and connected. Here's what you need to know.

children.text: The hearing aids of today bear little resemblance to the bulky, whistling devices of decades past. Modern hearing aids are sleek, nearly invisible, and packed with technology that adapts to your environment in real time.
Bluetooth connectivity
Most modern hearing aids can connect directly to smartphones, TVs, and other Bluetooth devices. This means you can stream phone calls, music, and audio directly to your hearing aids.

data.title: Tinnitus: Causes, Symptoms, and Treatment Options
data.excerpt: Ringing in the ears affects millions of Americans. Learn what causes tinnitus and how it can be treated.

children.text: Tinnitus — the perception of ringing, buzzing, hissing, or other sounds in the ears — affects an estimated 50 million Americans. While it can be distressing, many effective management strategies are available.
Common causes of tinnitus

Tinnitus can be caused by noise-induced hearing loss, age-related hearing loss, ear infections, earwax blockage, certain medications, or other medical conditions. Identifying the underlying cause is the first step to effective treatment.
